title: Scotland lineup vs. Haiti: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 Group C clash - Sports Mole
original_text:
Scotland have received a huge boost regarding the fitness of key midfielder Scott McTominay ahead of their opening Group C fixture against Haiti at the 2026 World Cup on Sunday.

The Napoli star was a notable absentee from training on Thursday due to a stomach complaint, before the precautionary decision was made for him to travel alone with a team doctor from their base camp in Charlotte.

However, McTominay was fit to take part in training on Friday and he should therefore be ready to start in midfield for Steve Clarke 's side, alongside either Lewis Ferguson or Kenny McLean. Ryan Christie and Man United teenager Tyler Fletcher, meanwhile, are alternatives options.

Billy Gilmour has been ruled out of the World Cup due to a knee injury, while defender Scott McKenna (knock) was absent from training again on Friday and is a doubt for Sunday's game.

Clarke is expected to line up with a 4-4-2 formation for this match and could rely on John McGinn - a Europa League winner with Aston Villa last month - and Ben Gannon-Doak to provide the width on the flanks.

Lawrence Shankland contributed to four of Scotland’s eight goals scored in warm-up victories against Curacao and Bolivia (three goals, one assists) and the new Rangers signing is likely to be rewarded with a start up front alongside Che Adams.

In defence, captain Andrew Robertson - who joined Tottenham last week - is set to start at left-back next to Grant Hanley, John Souttar and Aaron Hickey in a four-man defence, while Angus Gunn may get the nod to start in goal ahead of 43-year-olf Craig Gordon, who is the oldest player at the 2026 World Cup.
